public_reputation: Funeral-route knight who protects high-value body transfers and temple registrars.
appearance: Bright-plumed road gear, polished fittings, and visible attention to presentation mark the role before the voice does.
manner: Projects duty first, placing sworn role ahead of comfort, convenience, or easy compromise. In Panim, that reads as someone grave, measured, and ritual-aware, with constant awareness of consequence, grief, and divine mediation, and the habits of a knights still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of knights in and around Panim Haven, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Panim Haven workable through the obligations of knights while managing the immediate pressure of divine balance obligations in Panim.
hidden_pressure: Must keep oath, threshold, and public trust aligned even when expediency would be easier and more rewarding. Around Panim Haven, that usually means carrying divine balance obligations, sacrificial and mediation decorum, institutional scrutiny without letting the role of knights visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Templar Esh Navarre needs help holding a threshold, escorting the vulnerable, or investigating a breach of sworn protection near Panim Haven.
Working with Templar Esh Navarre can force the party to choose between expediency and the stricter obligations that govern Panim Haven.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Oath-signs, defensive bearing, and ritualized readiness make Templar Esh Navarre read as a sworn protector rather than a mere combatant. In Panim Haven, this lands inside a public field already shaped by House Panim.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Panim Haven: gates, sacred thresholds, escorts, or defended chambers
